findings.description: Duke Energy logo and tagline at bottom of title page missing in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows Duke Energy logo with 'BUILDING A SMARTER ENERGY FUTURE' tagline at bottom; HTML does not include this element

findings.description: Page 2 two-column layout collapsed to single column in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows NC DEQ content on left and SC DNR content on right in a two-column layout; HTML renders everything in a single column stacked vertically

findings.description: Partner logos grid layout collapsed to single column
findings.evidence: PDF shows partner logos (NC Wildlife, NC State, Duke Energy, UofSC, Catawba Riverkeeper, Charlotte Water, Storm Water, Lake James, Lake Norman, Lake Wylie, Lake Wateree, Catawba Wateree, Yadkin-Pee Dee) in a multi-column grid; HTML stacks them vertically

findings.description: Some partner logos appear cropped or partially visible in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows full logos for Lake Norman Marine Commission and others; HTML shows some logos cut off (e.g., 'Lake N Marine Co' truncated)

findings.description: Page 4 two-column layout collapsed - form on left, key takeaways on right in PDF
findings.evidence: PDF shows Aquatic Plant Report form on left side and Key takeaways bullet list on right side; HTML renders them stacked vertically

findings.description: Page 4 agency logos (DEQ and Wildlife Resources Commission) placement changed
findings.evidence: PDF shows DEQ and NC Wildlife Resources Commission logos side by side at bottom right; HTML shows them stacked vertically below the text
PDF shows good ID photos (green border) on left and bad ID photos (red border) on right in two columns; HTML stacks them vertically

findings.description: Photo border color-coding less clear in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF clearly shows green-bordered photos (good examples) vs red-bordered photos (bad examples); HTML preserves borders but layout change reduces the visual comparison effect

findings.description: Page 6 two-column layout collapsed - contact info on left, Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers poster on right
findings.evidence: PDF shows contact emails on left side and 'Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers' infographic on right side; HTML stacks them vertically

findings.description: Email addresses lose their distinctive color formatting in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows AquaticPlants@duke-energy.com and LakeServices@duke-energy.com in teal/cyan color; HTML renders them in plain dark text

findings.description: Multiple h2 headings used where the document only has one h1, but 'Key takeaways:' is rendered as h2 when it is not a section heading in the PDF - it's a label for a bullet list. This is minor. More importantly, there is a nested <main> inside <main>, which is a structural issue.
The HTML has a duplicate <main> wrapper - the outer <main> from the template and an inner <main> from the converted content. This is a structural duplication but does not duplicate visible content.

The PDF contains several URLs (duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants, AquaticPlants@duke-energy.com, LakeServices@duke-energy.com, www.protectyourwaters.net) but none of them are rendered as clickable hyperlinks in the HTML. They appear as plain text only.
findings.evidence:
PDF page 3: 'duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants' shown as a link. HTML: rendered as plain text '

duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants

' without an tag.

findings.description: Some detailed text visible in the PDF screenshots (e.g., the NC DEQ Aquatic Weed Control Program description text, the SC DNR noxious weed laws text on page 2, and the detailed text on the Aquatic Plant Management webpage on page 3) is not transcribed in the HTML. However, this content appears within embedded screenshots/images in the PDF, not as standalone text, so its omission is expected.
findings.evidence: Page 2 contains screenshots of NC DEQ and SCDNR websites with readable text about aquatic weed control programs and state laws. Page 3 contains a screenshot of the duke-energy.com website with descriptive paragraphs about aquatic plants in reservoirs.

findings.description: Some alt texts are reasonably descriptive but a few are somewhat generic or could be more precise. The Lake James Environmental Association logo is labeled as 'Belmont North Carolina Logo' and the Gastonia logo is labeled as such but may not be accurately identifiable from the PDF. However, these are partner logos on page 2 that are small and difficult to identify precisely.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt='Belmont North Carolina Logo' and alt='Gastonia Logo' for logos on page 2 that may or may not correspond to those entities.
